title: Container isolation for unattended runs
description: The loop currently runs on the bare Win11 host - the one open violation of the safety floor (improvements C1, highest-leverage safety item; adopt the textcortex/claude-code-sandbox Docker pattern). Run ADW_TICKET_RUN stage sessions inside a container with the repo mounted, no real credentials beyond a scoped git token, and network restricted to what stages need. This is the precondition for genuinely unattended operation and for any future --skip-permissions mode.
acceptance_criteria: Stage invocations run inside a container when an isolation flag is set; the host filesystem outside the repo mount is unreachable
No host credentials are visible inside the container beyond the scoped git push token
Hooks (PreToolUse guard, Stop checklist, autocommit) still function inside the container
A documented fallback runs stages on the host exactly as today when isolation is off
README documents the isolation setup and its limits
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green
